He added that U.S. and Israeli air forces now have uncontested control of Iranian airspace.

$ONDS subsidiary Airobotics secured a $20M order to deploy an AI-driven autonomous border security system for a national government.

$TSLA largest retail shareholder Leo KoGuan says he bought 1M shares of $NVDA and plans to buy more.

He says AI isn’t a bubble calling Tesla physical AI while Nvidia powers the foundational layer.

Absolutely wild to see the South Korean stock market KOSPI trigger back-to-back circuit breakers, yet people are wildly over-correlating it to U.S. markets.

This is largely about South Korea’s heavy exposure to LNG imports while Qatar’s output remains suspended but it has nothing to do with memory demand:

• SK Hynix and Samsung are sold out through 2026
• Memory prices are rising 40%+ this year
• $NVDA Rubin will require 288GB per GPU vs. Blackwell’s 192GB

Don’t confuse forced liquidation with memory hitting a top.

Tom Lee says the worst of the selloff should occur this week with markets stabilizing afterward.

He expects March to finish higher with Mag 7, software & crypto near a bottom

*CIRCUIT BREAKER TRIGGERED AFTER S. KOREA'S KOSPI PLUNGES 8%
*KOREA'S KOSPI EXTENDS DROP TO 10% AFTER TRADING RESUMES

The chart shows that the South Korean KOSPI stock index has doubled since last August (green) and has already corrected 17% from Friday's high (red), including a 10% correction today.

This market is not for the faint of heart.

South Korea's market is retail-dominated. It is estimated that up to 70% of its trading is retail, compared to about 20% for the NYSE.

For years, South Korea was the world's largest equity options market by contract volume, even larger than the United States. This is a remarkable fact for a mid-sized economy.

This is what retail-dominated markets do. They don't rally, they double. They don't correct, they crash.

South Korea imports 94% of its oil, with 75% coming from the Middle East. So, it is easy to see why its "degens" are panicking.

The SPX is now down ~2.5%.

As the red annotations show, the 10-year yield is 5 bps lower to 4.06%.

Bonds are being bought as crash insurance.

This probably means stocks have to crash to keep bonds from trading back to 4.11%.

Yesterday, the national average gasoline price jumped 11.2 cents (bottom panel).

This exceeded the largest one-day jump after the start of the Russia-Ukraine War in March 2022 of 10.9 cents.
 
Only the early September 20025 one-day jump of 19 cents was larger. This was when Category 5 Hurricane Katrina tore up oil infrastructure in the Gulf around New Orleans.
---
Keep in mind, this is the beginning of the month. So starting the month with a big daily jump in gasoline prices could lead to a huge increase in Energy CPI for March. 

Will it be rationalized away? Yes. But if you want the Fed to cut rates, things just got a lot more complicated if energy CPI pushes headline CPI to a big number in March.

Nebius $NBIS has received approval to construct its flagship data center in Missouri scaling up to 1.2GW, with reports suggesting the facility will cost $6.6 billion to construct with first operations expected by 2028.

$ASTS proving “Go to Status”

MARKET PANIC WITHOUT FOLLOW-THROUGH

Just 24 hours ago, fear was dominating sentiment. Many commentators were predicting a market crash after $SPY opened below the key $675 level, briefly falling to around $669. 

However, the market quickly proved those fears wrong.

Instead of continuing lower, the market reclaimed $671, signaling that downside momentum was fading.

If the market were truly going to collapse toward $655, it would have needed to stay below $675 and build sustained selling pressure. Instead, the market grinded higher, reclaiming $677 and reaching $682, effectively returning to the prior trading range.

So, despite headlines, volatility, and fear, the market failed to produce downside follow-through. Remember, price levels matter more than emotions.

This is the same reason why I did not trigger the “Blood in the Streets” account. 

I only start buying when $SPY falls 5% or more from its highs. The S&P is only about 3% off its highs, which means: there is no true “blood in the streets” environment, the real dip-buying strategy has not yet triggered, and the market remains within a normal correction or range.

If you enjoyed this video, please ❤️like and 🔁retweet

$SPX gapped down ~120 handles yesterday but reclaimed 6775 and pushed to 6840. Bears had their chance and we’re back in the range, but it’s very tricky with no real leadership. The range is now looser/wider, with 6710 as the low to use moving forward.
